summary: The main claim is: If you look at the ranked list of questions (Appendix C.1), the most relevant pattern found by the lie detector is this: LLMs which have just lied (have a lie in their context) are more likely to claim that lying is morally OK.
The fact that the factual questions work is also not surprising, because yeah, LLMs that have just lied are more likely to lie again.
The main claim is: How could creating an automated alignment researcher go wrong?
But we might need the automated alignment researcher to solve the Al alignment problem.
The main claim is: The Basel Accords are a set of banking standards (Basel I, II, and III) set by the Basel Committee on Bank Supervision (BCBS).
The main claim is: Just like many capabilities of GPT models were not discovered until after public release).
Doesn’t intelligence make people more moral?
The main claim is: If we want more people in the world who understand AGI safety, we need a place where it's accepted and encouraged to ask about the basics.
This is a safe space for people to ask stupid questions, so be kind!
